# kagiPro AI Full Index

This file provides machine-oriented repo guidance while keeping user docs narrative-first.

## 1) Package purpose
kagiPro provides R-native access to Kagi APIs with reproducible JSON output, parquet conversion, and optional corpus enrichment.

## 2) Public API map

### Connection
- `kagi_connection(api_key, base_url)`

### Query constructors
- `query_search(...)`
- `query_enrich_web(...)`
- `query_enrich_news(...)`
- `query_summarize(...)`
- `query_fastgpt(...)`

### Execution
- `kagi_request(connection, query, ...)` low-level request executor
- `kagi_fetch(connection, query, project_folder, ...)` high-level project-folder orchestrator

### Conversion/storage
- `kagi_request_parquet(input_json, output, ...)` JSON->parquet conversion
- `clean_request(project_folder, ...)` remove JSON payloads while preserving metadata
- `kagi_update_query(connection, project_folder, query_name, ...)` rerun query by name and refresh touched partitions

### Corpus pipeline
- `download_content(project_folder, endpoint = NULL, query_name = NULL, ...)`
- `content_markdown(project_folder, endpoint = NULL, query_name = NULL, ...)`
- `markdown_abstract(project_folder, endpoint = NULL, query_name = NULL, summarizer_fn, ...)`
- `summarize_with_openai(text, ...)`
- `summarize_with_kagi(text, ...)`
- `read_corpus(project_folder, endpoint, corpus = "parquet", abstracts = FALSE, ...)`

## 3) Folder contracts

### Request/parquet (project workflow)
- `<project>/<endpoint>/json`
- `<project>/<endpoint>/parquet`

### Corpus enrichment
- `<project>/<endpoint>/content/query=<query>`
- `<project>/<endpoint>/markdown/query=<query>`
- `<project>/<endpoint>/abstract/query=<query>`

## 4) Data contracts
- Query partition column: `query`
- Record identifier: `id`
- Abstract field: `abstract` (lowercase)
- Abstract linking in `read_corpus(..., abstracts = TRUE)`: by `id + query`

## 5) Error/robustness model
- `kagi_request(..., error_mode = "stop")`: fail-fast
- `kagi_request(..., error_mode = "write_dummy")`: continue with endpoint-compatible dummy payloads + warnings

## 6) Maintainer rules (repo)
- Long-lived branches: `main`, `dev`
- Do not delete `dev` after PR merges
- No branch deletion unless explicitly requested and explicitly confirmed
- Keep docs/tests/contracts synchronized with behavior changes in same change set
- Use detailed commit messages with behavior/docs/validation sections

## 7) Human docs and guides
- README: `README.md`
- Design context: `PROJECT_DESIGN.md`
- Skills: `inst/skills/README.md`
- Guides:
  - `vignettes/quickstart.qmd`
  - `vignettes/corpus-workflow.qmd`
  - `vignettes/search-endpoint.qmd`
  - `vignettes/enrich-endpoint.qmd`
  - `vignettes/summarize-endpoint.qmd`
  - `vignettes/fastgpt-endpoint.qmd`
